product: Custom Large Language Models (LLMs) for enterprise customers
audit_period: 8/26/2025 (certification agreement date; no observation period disclosed)
opinion: Not yet issued - this document is the Certification Agreement Form, not the final audit report or certificate
hq: 2261 Market Street STE 85861, San Francisco, CA 94114

system_description.overview: Parsed Systems, Inc. researches, develops, deploys, and manages custom large language models (LLMs) designed to be more cost-efficient, faster, and more intelligent for enterprise customers. The scope includes the design, engineering, and maintenance of proprietary AI models, training pipelines, and supporting infrastructure that securely process and optimize data for building domain-specific, high-performance language models.

products.Custom LLMs: Domain-specific, high-performance large language models for enterprise customers
products.AI Training Pipelines: Proprietary training pipelines and supporting infrastructure for building and optimizing LLMs

infrastructure.physical_presence: 2261 Market Street STE 85861, San Francisco, CA 94114 (single site under scope)
infrastructure._note: No infrastructure details disclosed in this certification agreement document

network_architecture.topology: No diagram described in report. This certification agreement contains no network architecture details.

authentication_access_control._note: No authentication or access control details disclosed

ci_cd_devops._note: No CI/CD or DevOps details disclosed

monitoring_logging._note: No monitoring or logging details disclosed

third_party_services.purpose: Accreditation body for the certification body

notable_controls: Document is a Certification Agreement Form with UAF endorsement letter, not the full ISO 27001 audit report or Statement of Applicability (SoA)
Scope references SoA Version 1.0 but the SoA itself is not included
Certification scope covers: IT Infrastructure Management, Human Resources, Physical Security, Legal & Compliance, Administrative Operations
Total audit mandays for certification cycle: 9 (Stage-1: 2, Stage-2: 3, Surveillance-1: 2, Surveillance-2: 2)
compliance_controls._note: No control testing results are present in this document. The actual audit report with control findings was not provided.

infrastructure.rationale: No infrastructure details disclosed whatsoever. The document is a certification agreement, not a technical audit report. Cannot assess cloud provider, regions, AZs, or any infrastructure component.

application_architecture.rationale: No application architecture details disclosed. We know the company builds custom LLMs with training pipelines but no technical specifics (languages, frameworks, compute, environments) are provided.

data_layer.rationale: No data layer details disclosed. For an AI/LLM company, data handling is critical, but nothing is revealed about databases, storage, backups, or data classification.

security_posture.rationale: The company is pursuing ISO 27001 certification, which implies an ISMS exists. However, no security tools, practices, or controls are described in this document. The pursuit of certification is a positive signal but provides no verifiable technical detail.

devops_maturity.rationale: No DevOps practices, CI/CD tools, IaC, or deployment processes disclosed.

bcdr_readiness.rationale: No BCDR information disclosed. No RTO/RPO, backup strategy, or disaster recovery plans mentioned.

vendor_diversity.rationale: Only the certification body (Gradient Certification Inc.) and accreditation body (UAF) are mentioned. No technology vendors, cloud providers, or service dependencies disclosed.

overall.rationale: This document is an ISO 27001 Certification Agreement Form and UAF endorsement letter -- not an audit report. It contains virtually no technical or security control information. The only useful data is the company identity, scope of certification, and the fact that they are pursuing ISO 27001. A meaningful tech due diligence assessment is not possible from this document alone.

red_flags: Document provided is NOT the ISO 27001 audit report -- it is only the Certification Agreement Form between Parsed Systems and Gradient Certification Inc.
No Statement of Applicability (SoA) included despite being referenced as Version 1.0
No audit findings, control testing results, or nonconformity details present
Zero technical infrastructure details disclosed for an AI/LLM company that presumably requires significant compute and data infrastructure
Only 7 pages total -- flagged as potentially incomplete per skill guidelines (fewer than 10 pages)
Auditor is Gradient Certification Inc. (Sheridan, WY / Houston, TX) accredited by UAF -- not a well-known Big 4 or major certification body
yellow_flags: Total audit mandays of 9 for entire 3-year certification cycle seems low for an AI/LLM company with training pipelines and infrastructure
Single site under scope (San Francisco address only) with no temporary/additional sites
No observation period or audit end date specified beyond agreement signing date of 8/26/2025
Certification agreement was just signed -- certification may not yet be granted
green_flags: Company is actively pursuing ISO 27001 certification, indicating commitment to information security governance
Scope of certification is comprehensive, covering core business functions plus IT Infrastructure Management, HR, Physical Security, Legal & Compliance, and Administrative Operations
ISMS applies to the full lifecycle: research, development, deployment, and management of LLMs
key_observations: This is a pre-certification document, not a completed audit report. The actual ISO 27001 certificate and audit findings are not available in this PDF.
The company builds custom enterprise LLMs -- a domain with significant data security, IP protection, and infrastructure requirements -- yet none of these are addressed in the provided document.
To perform meaningful tech due diligence, the actual ISO 27001 audit report, Statement of Applicability (SoA), and ideally a SOC 2 Type 2 report would be needed.
Gradient Certification Inc. uses UAF accreditation. UAF is a smaller accreditation body; most enterprise customers expect ANAB, UKAS, or similar well-established accreditation bodies.
The contact email for complaints goes to info@intercert.com (InterCert), suggesting Gradient Certification Inc. may operate under or be related to InterCert.
